sections.paragraph:
περίχωρος, ον pert. to being around an area, neighboring (Gen 19:28) quite predom. used as a subst. (οἱ περίχωροι ‘the neighbors’ Demosth. 19, 266; Plut., Cat. Maj. 351 [25, 3], Eum. 593 [15, 13]; Aelian, NA 10, 46; Cass. Dio 36, 33) ἡ π. (sc. γῆ; B-D-F §241, 1) region around, neighborhood (LXX, which also has τὸ περίχωρον and τὰ περίχωρα. Loanw. in rabb.) Mt 14:35; Mk 6:55 v.l.; Lk 4:14, 37; 7:17; Ac 14:6; 1 Cl 11:1; GPt 9:34. Used w. gen.: of a river, whose neighboring region to the right and left is designated as ἡ π.: ἡ π. τοῦ Ἰορδάνου (Gen 13:10f) Mt 3:5 (s. below); Lk 3:3. ἡ περίχωρος τῶν Γερασηνῶν the Gerasenes and the people living around them Lk 8:37. ὅλη ἡ π. τῆς Γαλιλαίας Mk 1:28 is either epexegetic gen. the whole region around, that is, Galilee or the region around Galilee (Mt understands it in the latter sense, and 4:24 inserted ὅλη ἡ Συρία for it); the epexegetical gen. is prob. exhibited in GJs 8:3 καθόλου τῆς π. τῆς Ἰουδαίας throughout the Judaean countryside (s. deStrycker ad loc.). By metonymy for the inhabitants Mt 3:5.—DELG s.v. χώρα. M-M.

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

predom. predom. = predominant(ly)

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

  1. sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

gen. gen. = genitive

prob. prob. = probable, probably

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

deStrycker deStrycker = ÉdeStrycker; s. GJs—List 1

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 808
